# Excel Formatting Standards

## Goal

- Create professional, consistent, and easy-to-read Excel documents.
- Improve readability for planning, reporting, tracking, estimation, and QA documentation.
- Apply a standardized visual style across all worksheets.

## Input

- Any spreadsheet containing tabular business, project, QA, tracking, estimation, reporting, or planning data.
- Worksheets containing headers, data rows, notes, comments, summaries, totals, and hyperlinks.

## Constraints

### Font and Text

- Use Calibri throughout the workbook.
- Worksheet title: Calibri, 18-20 pt, Bold.
- Section headers: Calibri, 14-16 pt, Bold.
- Column headers: Calibri, 11 pt, Bold.
- Data cells: Calibri, 11 pt.

### Layout

- Freeze the top row.
- Enable filtering on all data columns.
- Auto-fit column widths where appropriate.
- Enable word wrap for long text fields.
- Use top vertical alignment for all cells.
- Maintain consistent spacing and row heights.

### Colors and Styling

- Use a clean corporate appearance.
- Titles should use dark blue text.
- Section headers should use a light gray background with bold dark blue text.
- Column headers should be bold with subtle borders.
- Data cells should use white backgrounds with light borders.
- Use alternating row shading when appropriate to improve readability.

### Borders

- Use thin light-gray borders for data cells.
- Use stronger borders around major sections and summary areas.
- Add emphasis to totals and summary rows using borders and fill colors.

### Comments and Notes

- Preserve line breaks and bullet formatting.
- Enable text wrapping.
- Use top alignment.
- Allocate sufficient column width for longer descriptions and comments.

### Hyperlinks

- Use standard hyperlink formatting.
- Ensure links remain visible and readable.
- Enable text wrapping if link text is lengthy.

### Totals and Summary Sections

- Use bold formatting.
- Apply subtle highlighting to totals.
- Visually separate summary rows from detailed data.
- Clearly identify calculated values and estimations.

### Conditional Formatting

- Apply conditional formatting where it improves readability.
- Use color coding for priority, status, risk, or other ranking fields.
- Maintain consistent color usage throughout the worksheet.

### Print and Presentation

- Optimize for management reviews and stakeholder presentations.
- Use landscape orientation when appropriate.
- Repeat header rows on printed pages.
- Minimize unnecessary whitespace.
- Ensure worksheets remain professional when exported to PDF.

## Expected Output

- Clean, professional Excel workbook.
- Consistent formatting across all worksheets.
- Readable tables with clear headers and section separation.
- Proper alignment, spacing, and visual hierarchy.
- Management-ready reporting format suitable for planning, tracking, estimation, and QA documentation.
